title: Exploring Riesling in the Finger Lakes: a seminar
summary: Riesling is such an interesting grape variety. In the spirit of exploring the landscape of Riesling and trying to see
content:
Riesling is such an interesting grape variety. In the spirit of exploring the landscape of Riesling and trying to see where the Finger Lakes wines fit in, we travelled to the Konstantin Frank winery on the west side of Keuka Lake.

Konstantin was born in Odessa in Ukraine and became a noted viticulturist. When he arrived in the Finger Lakes, everyone said you can’t grow vinifera varieties here because of the cold winters. But he knew that it wasn’t too cold to grow vinifera here because of his experiences in Odessa. He started with Riesling, Chardonnay and Pinot Noir and ended up planting 66 different vinifera varieties.

Riesling was where it started, so this was a good place to hold this seminar at the FLXcursion with some interesting Rieslings poured in several flights. It was led by Stuart Pigott, Lisa Granick and Thomas Pastusak. This was a really good selection of Rieslings.

Dr Loosen Ürziger Würzgarten Riesling Spätlese 2024 Mosel, Germany
Wonderfully exotic, tropical, floral citrus fruit nose with a vital palate showing concentrated, off-dry lemon and lime fruit with good acidity countering the sweetness. This is a beautiful, intense Riesling with real power. Just gorgeous. 94/100

Robert Weil Kiedricher Gräfenberg Riesling Spätlese 2024 Rheingau, Germany
This is powerful and limey with keen acidity as well as some sweetness. Textured, pure and intense with amazing tension, and an almost perfect balance between the sweetness and the acidity. Lovely. 94/100

Dönhoff Niederhäuser Hermannshöle Riesling Spätlese 2024 Nahe, Germany (magnum)
This is concentrated with rich, off-dry citrus, pear and even some melon fruit, with nice acidity under the bold sweet fruit. Linear, with some sweetness and also good acidity, showing nice depth of fruit. Beautiful balance. Has a touch of chalkiness and saltiness on the finish. 94/100

Martin’s Lane Fritzi’s Vineyard Riesling 2025 Okanagan, Canada
This is concentrated and mineral with lovely tension to the crystalline citrus fruit, and a really nice floral twist. Concentrated, linear and mineral in the mouth, but also with some richness. Beautiful. 95/100

Galen Glen Stone Cellar Riesling 2024 Pennsylvania
Fresh, pure, dry and fruity with lovely melon and pear notes as well as some citrus. Nice texture and acidity with lovely flow across the palate. Crystalline finish. 93/100

Eva Fricke Lorch Dry Riesling 2024 Rheingau, Germany
This is concentrated and bold, and dry. Lovely focused citrus fruit with a bit of structure and nice acidity. Really fine and well balanced with good concentration, and a stony, mineral undercurrent. Finishes with good acidity. Lovely tapering finish. 95/100

AJ Adam Goldtröpfen Riesling Grosses Gewächs 2023 Mosel, Germany
There’s some depth here with pear and peach richness and just a touch of sweetness as well as crystalline citrus and a long finish where the acidity becomes apparent. So lovely with richness but also restraint. 94/100

Knewitz Hoingberg Riesling Grosses Gewächs 2024 Rheingau, Germany
Limestone. This is fresh and supple, with lovely generous fruit, showing crystalline citrus, nice spiciness, and good acidity. Lovely precision but also some pear, melon and apple depth. Lovely texture to this Riesling. 93/100

Weingut Heinrich Spindler Jesuitengarten Riesling Grosse Lage 2023 Pfalz, Germany
Dry and quite intense with bold limey fruit and nice structure and acidity. So tensile with high acid framing the generous fruit. Apple, lime and pear. Really full and quite intense. 94/100

Dr Bürklin-Wolf Gaisböhl Riesling Grosses Gewachs 2023 Pfalz, Germany
Lovely flesh here (melon, ripe pear) as well as some linear citrus fruit. There’s also a bit of structure. Juicy and with nice focus, this is juicy and fine with lovely depth. 94/100

Weingut Sommer Riesling When We See Edition No 1 2023 Leithaberg, Austria
This is so textural and alive, with bold crystalline citrus fruit and some richer melon notes, finishing crisp and spicy with some mineral detail. Crisp and energetic, and utterly delicious with some depth. 94/100

Nikolaihof Baumpresse Riesling 2015 Wachau, Austria
This is pressed in an old wooden press that’s 350 years old. Apple, pear and melon with some honey, but also chalky notes and then some citrus on the finish. So refined and sophisticated. This is deliciously layered, and has aged beautifully. 95/100

Forge Breakneck Creek Vineyard Dry Riesling 2023 Finger Lakes, New York
Concentrated and bold with vivid citrus fruit and a touch of pear and peach richness. There’s nice extract and depth here with good concentration of fruit. Slight salinity on the finish, and also a mineral streak. 94/100

Boundary Breaks Riesling Dry #239 2023 Finger Lakes, New York
Taut, bright, focused and aromatic with bold lemony fruit. Very pure with nice focus and intensity here. So juicy and pure, showing some richness to the citrus fruit. Very stylish. 92/100

Apollo’s Praise Pentecostal Block Dry Riesling 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
This is so beautifully textured with pear, peach and lemon, as well as some spicy detail. This has concentration and poise, with a hint of apricot richness. So expressive, showing generosity as well as tension. 95/100

Hillick & Hobbs Lower Terrace Riesling 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
This has depth and poise with some subtle oak influence, with nice concentration and depth. Lovely crystalline citrus fruit. Has a nice acid line, too. There’s such depth and focus to this wine. Really expressive with nice personality and depth. 94/100

title: Konstantin Frank, one of the most important wineries in the Finger Lakes region of New York State
summary: This is one of the most well known (and important) of the Finger Lakes wineries, with a debut vintage of
content:
This is one of the most well known (and important) of the Finger Lakes wineries, with a debut vintage of 1962. Konstantin Frank came to the USA from Ukraine after the war speaking no English. He’d been a viticulturist in Odessa, and there he was able to grow vinifera despite the freezing winters. In the Finger Lakes, people thought the winters were too cold for vinifera, but Konstantin knew he’d be able to do it, and he started with Riesling. He went on to plant 60 different varieties of vinifera.

For the full story, read this report.

I tasted these wines as part of the FLXcursion in the Finger Lakes region.  

Konstantin Frank Riesling Nature 2021 Keuka Lake, Finger Lakes, New York
This is traditional method with no dosage. Stony and bright with nice citrus drive. Has good acidity and a bit of grip. 91/100

Konstantin Frank Estate Blanc de Blancs 2021 Keuka Lake, Finger Lakes, New York
Four years on lees. Fresh and refined with crystalline citrus with some lemon and lime and a touch of spice. Vivid and bright with some pithy hints, finishing quite dry. 92/100

Konstantin Frank Dry Riesling 2025 Keuka Lake, Finger Lakes, New York
Floral and fruity with expressive citrus fruit and a floral edge. Nice texture on the palate. This is supple and textural. Fine, light and lovely. 93/100

Konstantin Frank Ethos 2025 Keuka Lake, Finger Lakes, New York
12% alcohol. This is 60% Riesling and 40% Rkatsiteli. Supple and rounded with transparent pear and citrus fruit. This has elegance and poise with a finely spiced finish. 92/100

Konstantin Frank Semi Dry Riesling 2025 Finger Lakes, New York
This has 25 g/l residual sugar. This comes from a warmer site, Hector, which is between Cayuga and Seneca Lake, with deep topsoil. Richly textured and just off-dry with nice balance and good palate weight. 93/100

Konstantin Frank Eugenia Riesling 2020 Keuka Lake, Finger Lakes, New York (magnum)
This vineyard was planted in 1958, and has very little top soil. Rich, stony and mineral. This has nice pear and apple fruit with some lemon, showing nice depth. Stony detail on the finish. 94/100

title: Visiting Hillick & Hobbs with winemaker Lynne Fahy – one of the most spectacular vineyards in the Finger Lakes region of New York State
summary: Website: https://www.hillickandhobbs.com/ Paul Hobbs is very well known in the wine world, and as well as consulting widely he opened
content:
Website: https://www.hillickandhobbs.com/

Paul Hobbs is very well known in the wine world, and as well as consulting widely he opened his first winery in 1981 after studying at UC Davis. He now has seven wineries worldwide. The latest addition to the portfolio is Hillick and Hobbs, on the east side of Seneca lake close to the town of Watkins Glen.

Paul grew up in Buffalo NY, so he’s from upstate New York, and his parents, Joan Hillick and Edward Hobbs met at Cornell University in Ithaca, about 30 minutes’ drive from the vineyard he named after them. He’s always been a lover of Riesling, and that’s what drew him back home to start this project.

First plantings were in 2014, with 2.5 acres. Then they planted 12 acres at once in 2015, and came unstuck with some erosion problems (heavy rains took some of the soil from the steeply sloping vineyards into the lake). They have slowly added extra vineyards since this big burst, and they now have 25 acres of Riesling that are now producing and have 1 acre of Grüner Veltliner.

Seneca Lake is the reason they can grow grapes here. It’s the deepest of the Finger Lakes, and holds as much water as the other 10 Finger Lakes combined. It’s 440 ft deep here, and at its deepest point it is 660 ft.

I tasted and walked the vineyards with winemaker Lynne Fahy, whose first vintage here was in 2021 (the first Hillick and Hobbs vintage was 2019). Lynne is originally from Rochester, and as well as working at Ravines and some other Finger Lakes wineries, she had a lengthy spell working in Oregon at Trisaetum.

There’s nothing magical about the winemaking approach. It relies on wild ferments on heavy juice lees for all the wines.

Lynne presses, settles over night, then ferments. The target NTUs are 400-500, which is high. She then leaves wines on gross lees. Lynne says she likes a little reduction in the young wines. Target YANs are 165 so they might add some Fermaid, but not DAP, if they are much below that.

There is a bit of oak use here. Each year they get some used 500 litre casks from the California winery, previously used for Chardonnay. They also have a 1000 litre fuder from Germany.

Hillick & Hobbs Estate Dry Riesling 2021 Seneca Lake, New York
5 g/l rs. A challenging vintage with lots of rain and then warm conditions during harvest. The complication was that it rained every day and it was warm at night, so there wasn’t a diurnal shift and the disease pressure was really high. But they managed to get very clean fruit in the end. They began picking October 12. This is concentrated, structured and quite bold with a spicy edge to the citrus fruit. Ripe with some apple and peach, as well as juicy citrus fruit. This is quite lovely. 93/100

Hillick & Hobbs Estate Dry Riesling 2022 Seneca Lake, New York
7 g/l rs. The opposite of 2021, with no rain from May to September. Just 1 ton/acre yields with small clusters, tiny berries. Lynne did press fractions this year because she was afraid of bitterness, and this has led to a really well balanced wine. Pristine and pure with lovely linear crystalline lemony fruit with nice precision and intensity. This has a pure, focused citrus fruit, with real concentration and a slight salty finish. This is quite beautiful. 95/100

Hillick & Hobbs Estate Dry Riesling 2023 Seneca Lake, New York
4 g/l rs, 5% oak. This was a typical Finger Lakes vintage with quite a bit of wine in July giving high downy mildew pressure, but it was dry in August. Great conditions for harvest, after it cooled down. Juicy, bright and linear with nice acidity as well as lovely focused citrus fruit. There’s some crystalline quality, and a touch of apple and grapefruit, together with some wet stone minerality. Such precision here. Tight and youthful. 94/100

Hillick & Hobbs Estate Dry Riesling 2024 Seneca Lake, New York
A very warm vintage, one of the hottest. But harvest time wasn’t hot. This is pristine and pure with amazing precision, showing bright lemon and lime fruit. Crystalline and linear with a lovely salty twist to the pure fruit. Still quite primary, dry, and with a bright future to it. Hints of sage and some nice floral detail. 95/100

Hillick & Hobbs Estate Dry Riesling Lower Terrace 2024 Seneca Lake, New York
Barrel fermented in 500 litre oak. There’s some depth and intensity here, as well as some creamy detail. Lemon, lime, pear and green apple character as well as some warm spiciness in the background. Zippy and alert with amazing presence, and a long, finely spiced finish. 94/100

Hillick & Hobbs Estate Dry Riesling North Ravine 2024 Seneca Lake, New York
Some cream and toast on the nose, as well as focused citrus fruit, as well as some pear and peach. Bright and mineral, but also showing some depth from the barrel fermentation. So expressive and energetic with a fine tapering finish. This is a really distinctive, delicious Riesling. 94/100

Hillick & Hobbs Estate Dry Riesling Klone 90 2024 Seneca Lake, New York
Second use 1000 litre German fuder, clone 90. This is quite beautiful. It’s powerful and balanced, with apricot, pear, lime and lemon. Crystalline and expressive with some spiciness, and real complexity to the fruit. Such precision and an eternal, finely spiced finish. Beautiful wine. 96/100

Hillick & Hobbs Botrytis Cinarea Estate Riesling 2024 Seneca Lake, New York
9.5 % alcohol. 90 g/l sugar. Picked October 2024. This is sweet and delicious with pear and peach as well as some table grape, with freshness. Very attractive and pure, and not overly sweet, so quite versatile. 93/100
